projects.name: Tk'emlúps te Secwépemc Nation Housing – Kamloops
projects.blurb: Canada announced over $20.1 million through the Affordable Housing Fund to build 58 units for the Tk'emlúps te Secwépemc Nation near Kamloops, BC, now officially open.

projects.name: Saskatchewan Supportive Housing – Brain Injuries and Seniors
projects.blurb: The Government of Saskatchewan invested $5 million through the Rental Development Program to open supportive housing for individuals with brain injuries and seniors experiencing homelessness.

projects.name: Regina Large-Family Affordable Housing Upgrades
projects.developer: Government of Canada / Government of Saskatchewan / CMHC
projects.blurb: A $3.35 million joint investment has upgraded housing units in Regina to provide large families in need with greater access to affordable homes.

projects.name: Regent Park Affordable Rental Homes – Toronto
projects.blurb: The federal government announced over $86 million to break ground on 271 affordable, secure rental homes in Toronto's Regent Park neighbourhood.

projects.name: Saint John Affordable Housing Development
projects.blurb: A new housing development in Saint John, New Brunswick will feature dozens of affordable units, with construction expected to be completed by fall 2026.

projects.name: Whistler Workforce and Family Housing
projects.blurb: A new housing project near public transit in Whistler began construction in June 2025, targeting local workers and families, with completion estimated for late 2027.

projects.name: The Beaches Purpose-Built Rental – Toronto (Cannect Homes)
projects.blurb: Cannect Homes and the City of Toronto broke ground on a new mixed-income, purpose-built rental building in the Beaches neighbourhood, converting a previously proposed condo project.

projects.name: British Columbia Affordable Housing Fund – 117 Units
projects.blurb: Canada announced over $20.8 million through the Affordable Housing Fund to build 117 affordable units across British Columbia.

projects.name: St. John's Affordable Home Repairs – CMHC
projects.blurb: Canada announced funding to repair 130 deeply affordable and community housing units in St. John's for low-income households, in partnership with private market developers.

projects.name: Army Street Micro-Homes – St. John's
projects.blurb: The Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation issued an RFP to build 10 micro-homes on Army Street in St. John's to address affordable housing needs.

projects.name: Chatham-Kent Affordable Homes Announcement
projects.blurb: The federal government committed funding to support 50 new affordable homes in Chatham-Kent as part of Canada's broader commitment of over 50,300 units nationwide.

projects.name: Lake Loon Rental Homes – Halifax
projects.blurb: Federal funding has been announced to provide 8 safe, affordable rental homes for residents in the Lake Loon area of Halifax Regional Municipality.

projects.name: Saint John Affordable Homes – CMHC Investment
projects.blurb: The Government of Canada announced more than $48 million to support the construction or repair of over 850 affordable homes in Saint John, New Brunswick.

projects.name: Northern Ontario Affordable Homes – CMHC Investment
projects.blurb: The Government of Canada announced more than $40 million in contributions and low-cost loans to build and repair 149 homes across Northern Ontario.

projects.name: Toronto Affordable Rental Homes – $137M CMHC Announcement
projects.blurb: The federal government announced over $137 million to help build 245 secure, affordable rental homes in Toronto, with the property located at a specific Toronto address.

projects.name: Quebec City Social and Affordable Housing – 280+ Units
projects.blurb: CMHC and the Société d'habitation du Québec announced over 280 new social and affordable housing units in Quebec City to support low- and moderate-income residents.

projects.name: Windsor Rapid Housing Initiative
projects.blurb: The federal government is providing over $3.6 million to rapidly build 12 new affordable homes in Windsor, Ontario through the Rapid Housing Initiative.

projects.name: Yellowhead Tribal Council Belvedere Affordable Housing Project
projects.blurb: Two four-storey buildings will deliver 149 mixed-income units to support First Nation families. The project is listed on Alberta's major projects registry.

projects.name: Williams Hall Apartments
projects.blurb: The Williams Hall Apartments project will restore the historic Williams Hall building and create 90 residential apartments along with amenity, office, and commercial space.

projects.name: Southview Site Redevelopment – Calgary
projects.developer: City of Calgary / Province of Alberta / Calgary Housing Company
projects.blurb: The City of Calgary, Province of Alberta, and Calgary Housing Company are jointly exploring the redevelopment potential of the Southview site to deliver affordable housing.
